title: carex rara subsp. capillacea
intro: Carex rara subsp. capillacea, also known as the Alpine Sedge, is a species of sedge native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Carex rara subsp. capillacea include its ability to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, as well as its potential to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
